steps.title: Test the all-species claim
steps.content: The conclusion is that none of the three countries' timber industries grow all 23 non-native tree species. The supporting graph data must show that every country's reported count is below 23.

steps.title: Find the upper bound
steps.content: Choice A discusses native species outside the 23-species set. Choice B discusses species not among the 23 being evaluated. Choice C says each country reported at least 4 species, which does not rule out all 23. The remaining choice says the highest-reporting country reported fewer than 15, so no country reached 23. The answer is D.
